decision: Always a card — Capturing vertebra with Clearing API consumption contract

steps.action: Spec consumption contract — what shape Capturing needs from Clearing transcripts and index
steps.notes: Consumption contract spec complete. Three extraction types: decisions (DECISION markers), commitments (I'll... patterns), action items (role-directed verbs). Intake queue design: post-session parser → ~/.chorus/intake/ JSON → Wren reviews → cards created. Does not replace /werk create — that's still manual for sequenced work. Open questions: auto-expiry, auto-run vs manual trigger, Wren approval authority.

steps.action: Build Capturing parser and intake queue against Silas spec
steps.notes: Capturing parser + intake queue built. chorus-capture.sh extracts decisions (DECISION markers), commitments (I'll/I'm going to patterns), and action items (role-directed verbs) from Clearing return JSON. Writes to ~/.chorus/intake/ as structured JSON per Silas spec. Session-start.sh wired to surface pending count. Tested against all 14 existing transcripts — 12 decisions, 43 commitments, 5 action items extracted total. CLI: --list, --count. Symlinked to ~/.chorus/scripts/.

steps.action: Validate card routing tags and lead time tracking
steps.notes: Validated capture parser output. 14 transcripts processed: 12 decisions, 43 commitments, 5 action items extracted. Routing tags working — commitments correctly attributed to roles (wren, silas, kade). Two gaps to close: (1) decisions need auto-card creation not just extraction, (2) lead time tracking needs timestamps from capture→card→completion to measure. Parser is solid foundation — routing tags validated, ready for Jeff to approve as 5th vertebra.

Jeff approves Capturing as 5th spine vertebra. Intake pipeline: Clearing transcripts → parser → intake queue → Wren reviews → cards. Consumption contract specced by Silas, parser built by Kade, routing validated by Wren. Capturing joins Directing, Designing, Building, Proving in the Chorus value stream.
